name: ZOLLINGER-ELLISON SYNDROME
definition: peptic ulcer diathesis ← marked hypersecretion of gastric acid ← gastrin-secreting islet cell

demographics.Location: duodenal bulb (65%) + stomach (20%), jejunum near ligament of Treitz (25%),

distribution: duodenal bulb (65%) + stomach (20%), jejunum near ligament of Treitz (25%),
discriminating_features: prominence of areae gastricae ← hyperplasia of parietal cells
enlarged thickened rugal folds in gastric fundus + upper body

general: prominence of areae gastricae ← hyperplasia of parietal cells
enlarged thickened rugal folds in gastric fundus + upper body
"wet stomach" = dilution of barium with poor coating by excess secretions in nondilated
dilatation of duodenum + upper small bowel ← fluid overload
thickened folds in duodenum + jejunum ← edema
